instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the play: Underground Sea, Polluted Delta, Brainstorm, Ponder, Force of Will, Daze, Murktide Regent?
output: This is a strong keep. You have 2 lands including a fetchland for Brainstorm shuffling, both cantrips to find more lands and threats, Force of Will plus Daze for free interaction, and Murktide Regent as a win condition. The hand has everything Dimir Tempo wants: mana, cantrips, disruption, and a finisher. On the play, Daze is at its best. Play Underground Sea, cast Ponder to set up your next draws. This hand is a 9/10.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the draw: Island, Wasteland, Force of Will, Force of Will, Daze, Daze, Orcish Bowmasters?
output: This is a risky but keepable hand. You have 4 pieces of interaction but only 1 colored mana source and 1 Wasteland (colorless). You need to draw a second blue source to cast Bowmasters and the second Daze. However, Force of Will and Daze protect you through turn 2, and if you draw any blue land or fetchland, the hand is excellent. On the draw, you get an extra card. Mulligan if you are risk-averse, keep if you trust your draws. This is a 6/10 hand.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand: Dark Ritual, Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, Lion's Eye Diamond, Burning Wish, Rite of Flame, Ponder?
output: This is a snap keep in The EPIC Storm. You can win turn 1: Ponder to set up your library (or skip it), Lotus Petal for R, Rite of Flame for RR, Dark Ritual for BBB, Dark Ritual for BBB, Lion's Eye Diamond (crack in response to Burning Wish for BBB), Burning Wish for Tendrils of Agony. That is 7 spells cast, so Tendrils with storm 7 drains for 16. You might need to sequence slightly differently, but this hand kills turn 1 through anything except Force of Will. Absolute snap keep, 10/10.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand: Plains, Plains, Plains, Thalia Guardian of Thraben, Stoneforge Mystic, Flickerwisp, Swords to Plowshares?
output: This is a good keep for Death and Taxes. You have 3 lands to curve out, Thalia on turn 2 (taxes all their spells), Stoneforge on turn 2 (tutors Batterskull), Flickerwisp for value, and Swords for removal. The only concern is no Aether Vial (your best card) and no Wasteland or Rishadan Port for mana denial. But the creature curve is strong and Thalia on turn 2 against blue decks is game-warping. Keep. 7/10.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand: Volcanic Island, Scalding Tarn, Delver of Secrets, Delver of Secrets, Lightning Bolt, Lightning Bolt, Lightning Bolt?
output: This is a trap hand. It looks aggressive but it is actually terrible. Two Delvers with no cantrips means you have no way to set up Delver flips and they will stay 1/1s. Three Bolts with no Force of Will or Daze means any combo deck kills you before your Bolts matter. You have zero card selection to improve the hand. Against combo: you lose. Against fair decks: you probably still lose because Delver does not flip and Bolt only does 9 damage total. Mulligan to 6. 3/10.

instruction: Should I mulligan this 6-card hand: Polluted Delta, Underground Sea, Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, Force of Will, Orcish Bowmasters?
output: Keep this 6-card hand immediately. It has everything: 2 lands including a fetchland, Brainstorm for card selection, Thoughtseize for proactive disruption, Force of Will for free countermagic, and Bowmasters as a versatile threat. This 6-card hand is better than most 7-card hands. Thoughtseize turn 1 takes their best card, Bowmasters turn 2, then Brainstorm with fetchland to find more gas. 9/10 for a mulligan to 6.

instruction: Board state: I have Murktide Regent (8/8 flyer), opponent has Marit Lage token (20/20 flying indestructible). Who is winning?
output: Your opponent is winning. Marit Lage is a 20/20 flying indestructible token that kills in one attack. Your 8/8 Murktide Regent cannot block it (both have flying, but Marit Lage has 20 power to your 8 toughness so it kills Murktide and still deals 12 to you). You need to either: 1) Race by attacking with Murktide and removing their blockers (you need 3 attacks, they need 1). 2) Bounce Marit Lage with Karakas (if legendary - it is not, so this does not work). 3) Use Swords to Plowshares or similar exile effect. 4) Counter the Dark Depths or Thespian's Stage activation before it creates the token. If the token exists and you have no exile removal, you have approximately one turn to win.

instruction: Board state: I have Thalia Guardian of Thraben, my opponent has 3 cards in hand and 2 Islands untapped. Should I play my Stoneforge Mystic?
output: Yes, you should play Stoneforge Mystic. Your opponent has 2 Islands untapped. With Thalia in play, their counterspells cost 1 more. Force of Will normally costs a pitch card and 1 life, but with Thalia it costs a pitch card, 1 life, AND 1 mana. They have 2 mana, so they CAN pay for Force. However, if they Force your Stoneforge, they spend 2 cards (Force + pitch) to stop your 2-drop. That is a good trade for you since you still have Thalia attacking. If they do not counter, you get Batterskull. Play Stoneforge. Either outcome is favorable.

instruction: Board state: My opponent played Show and Tell. I have Containment Priest in hand. When do I put it into play?
output: Put Containment Priest into play with Show and Tell itself. Show and Tell says each player MAY put a permanent from their hand into play. Containment Priest enters the battlefield first (simultaneously with their creature). Containment Priest says: if a nontoken creature would enter the battlefield and it was not cast, exile it instead. Since their Emrakul or Atraxa is entering via Show and Tell (not being cast), Containment Priest exiles it. This is one of the best Show and Tell counter-strategies. The timing works because both permanents enter simultaneously and Containment Priest's replacement effect applies.

instruction: Board state: I have Painter's Servant naming blue in play. My opponent casts Abrupt Decay targeting it. Can I counter with Red Elemental Blast?
output: No, you cannot. Abrupt Decay says 'this spell can't be countered.' Red Elemental Blast says 'counter target blue spell' and with Painter naming blue, Abrupt Decay is indeed blue. However, Abrupt Decay's 'can't be countered' clause overrides your ability to counter it. Your Red Elemental Blast would be a legal target (it targets a blue spell), but on resolution it would fail to counter Abrupt Decay. Your Painter's Servant will be destroyed. You need to protect it with a different method, such as giving it hexproof or bouncing it in response.

instruction: Board state: I control Blood Moon. My opponent plays a fetchland. Can they activate it?
output: Yes, they can activate the fetchland, but it will not do what they want. Under Blood Moon, their fetchland enters the battlefield as a Mountain (it loses all abilities except being a Mountain). However, if they ALREADY had the fetchland before Blood Moon resolved, it is now a Mountain with no activated abilities. They cannot sacrifice it to search. If they play a new fetchland after Blood Moon is in play, it enters as a Mountain immediately and cannot be activated. The key timing is: fetchlands already in play when Moon resolves lose their abilities. New lands enter as Mountains.

instruction: Board state: I have Delver of Secrets (1/1) and Wasteland. Opponent has Underground Sea and Tropical Island. Should I Wasteland now or wait?
output: It depends on what your opponent's deck is doing. If they only have these 2 lands and no other mana sources, Wasteland the Underground Sea NOW. This cuts them off black mana entirely (Tropical only produces UG), stranding Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Bowmasters in hand. If they have a fetchland or other blue source, wait - they can still cast blue spells and your Wasteland is better saved for a utility land like Urza's Saga. The general Wasteland heuristic: destroy the land that cuts them off a color, or destroy the most impactful utility land. Do not Wasteland a basic.

instruction: Board state: Both players at 10 life. I have 2 Lightning Bolts in hand, opponent has Force of Will in hand (I saw it from Gitaxian Probe). Can I win?
output: You have 6 damage from 2 Bolts, but opponent is at 10 life, so you need 4 more damage from somewhere. However, if they Force of Will one Bolt, you only deal 3 damage and they pay 1 life for Force (going to 9). Key question: do they have a blue card to pitch? If yes, they can Force one Bolt and you deal only 3. If their Force of Will is the only blue card, they cannot use it. The line: cast Bolt targeting them. If they Force, they go to 9 (10 - 1 life for Force). Then cast second Bolt for 3 (they go to 6). Not lethal either way from 10. You need another source of damage first.

instruction: Board state: I have Chalice of the Void on 1. Opponent casts Force of Negation pitching a blue card (alternative cost). Is Force countered?
output: No, Force of Negation is NOT countered by Chalice on 1. Chalice of the Void counters spells with the specified mana value. Force of Negation has a mana value of 3 (its printed mana cost is 1UU = 3), regardless of how it was cast. The alternative cost of exiling a blue card changes how you pay for it but does NOT change its mana value. Chalice on 1 only counters spells with mana value 1. To counter Force of Negation with Chalice, you would need Chalice on 3. Same applies to Force of Will (mana value 5) - Chalice on 1 does not stop it.

instruction: Board state: I have Leyline of the Void in play from the start of the game. Opponent casts Reanimate targeting a creature in their graveyard. What happens?
output: Reanimate cannot target a creature in your opponent's graveyard because there are no creatures in their graveyard. With Leyline of the Void in play, cards that would go to your opponent's graveyard go to exile instead. This means creatures that die, are discarded, or are milled are all exiled. When your opponent tries to cast Reanimate, there are no legal targets in their graveyard. Reanimate requires a target on cast, and without one, it cannot be cast at all. This is why Leyline of the Void is the #1 sideboard card against Reanimator.

instruction: Board state: Opponent has Emrakul, the Aeons Torn in play via Sneak Attack. It is their combat phase. I have Karakas untapped. Can I bounce Emrakul?
output: Yes! Karakas can bounce Emrakul. Karakas says 'tap: return target legendary creature to its owner's hand.' Emrakul has protection from colored spells, but Karakas is a land ability, not a spell. Protection does not prevent abilities of permanents from targeting or affecting a creature. Activate Karakas before combat damage is dealt (during the declare attackers step or declare blockers step). Emrakul returns to hand, no combat damage is dealt, and the annihilator trigger has already happened if you waited until after attackers. To avoid annihilator entirely, bounce BEFORE they declare attackers in the beginning of combat step.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand for Sneak and Show: Ancient Tomb, Island, Show and Tell, Emrakul, Force of Will, Brainstorm, Ponder?
output: Excellent keep. Ancient Tomb + Island = 3 mana on turn 2 for Show and Tell. Emrakul is your payoff. Force of Will protects the combo (you can pitch Brainstorm or Ponder if needed). If they have disruption, Brainstorm and Ponder dig for alternatives. This hand has combo, protection, and backup plan. 9/10.

instruction: Should I keep this Oops All Spells hand: Lotus Petal, Dark Ritual, Elvish Spirit Guide, Balustrade Spy, Pact of Negation, Force of Will, Narcomoeba?
output: Snap keep - this wins on turn 1. Sequence: Elvish Spirit Guide (exile for G... wait, you need B or colorless for Spy). Lotus Petal for B, Dark Ritual for BBB, cast Balustrade Spy (costs 3B) using BBB+B. Spy mills your entire library. Narcomoeba triggers. Pact of Negation and Force of Will protect against opponent's Force of Will. This is a protected turn-1 kill. 10/10.

instruction: Should I keep this Lands hand: Exploration, Mox Diamond, Dark Depths, Thespian's Stage, Wasteland, Life from the Loam, Crop Rotation?
output: Incredible keep. Turn 1: Mox Diamond (discard Wasteland), Exploration, play Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. Turn 2: Activate Stage copying Depths (both have 0 ice counters), create Marit Lage 20/20. You have Crop Rotation and Life from the Loam as backup, plus the discarded Wasteland can be dredged back with Loam. This is a turn-2 kill hand with redundancy. 10/10.

instruction: Should I keep this Death and Taxes hand: Aether Vial, Thalia, Stoneforge Mystic, Phyrexian Revoker, Karakas, Plains, Plains?
output: Very strong keep. Turn 1: Plains, Aether Vial. Turn 2: Plains, Thalia (noncreature spells cost 1 more). Turn 3: Vial on 2, play Stoneforge (tutor Batterskull), Vial in Phyrexian Revoker naming their best activated ability. Karakas protects your legends and bounces opposing legends. This hand has the perfect D&T curve: Vial into taxing creature into equipment. 9/10.

instruction: Should I keep this Storm hand: Dark Ritual, Dark Ritual, Burning Wish, Brainstorm, Ponder, Underground Sea, no other lands?
output: Risky but keepable. You have a 1-land hand with 2 cantrips to find more mana. Dark Ritual + Dark Ritual + Underground Sea = BBBBBBB (7 black). Burning Wish costs 2 mana. That leaves 5 mana floating. But you need more storm count for lethal Tendrils (storm needs ~9 copies for 20 damage). The cantrips help find Lion's Eye Diamond or more rituals. On the play, keep - cantrips on turn 1 find your second land. On the draw, borderline - opponent might disrupt you first. 6/10 on play, 5/10 on draw.

instruction: Should I keep this Painter hand: Ancient Tomb, Mountain, Painter's Servant, Grindstone, Goblin Welder, Pyroblast, Red Elemental Blast?
output: Good keep. Turn 1: Ancient Tomb, Painter's Servant (naming blue). Turn 2: Mountain, Grindstone (2 to cast), activate Grindstone (3 total): opponent's entire library is milled since everything is blue. You have Goblin Welder as backup if Painter is removed, and Pyroblast + REB as protection that becomes unconditional countermagic with Painter in play. The only risk is opponent having Force of Will for Painter on turn 1. 8/10.

instruction: Should I keep this Eldrazi hand: Ancient Tomb, Eldrazi Temple, Chalice of the Void, Chalice of the Void, Thought-Knot Seer, Reality Smasher, Wasteland?
output: Strong keep. Turn 1: Ancient Tomb, Chalice of the Void on 1 (shuts out cantrips, Swords, Bolt, Thoughtseize). Turn 2: Eldrazi Temple, Thought-Knot Seer (4 mana from Tomb + Temple + Temple's Eldrazi bonus). You strip their best card and have a 4/4. Turn 3: Reality Smasher with the second Chalice as backup for a different number if needed. Wasteland handles problematic lands. Two Chalice is slightly redundant but the turn-1 Chalice is devastating. 8/10.

instruction: Board state: I have Delver of Secrets (still a 1/1, hasn't flipped). Top card of my library is Lightning Bolt (I know from Ponder). Should I flip Delver or cast another spell?
output: Let Delver flip. During your upkeep, reveal Lightning Bolt from the top - it is an instant, so Delver transforms into Insectile Aberration (3/2 flyer). A 3/2 flyer is a 7-turn clock becoming a 4-turn clock. The Bolt stays on top for you to draw next turn. Do not cast a spell before upkeep that would change the top card (like cracking a fetchland). Sequencing: upkeep flip Delver, draw Bolt, then decide whether to use Bolt this turn or hold it.

instruction: Board state: Opponent has Blood Moon in play. I have a Polluted Delta in hand. Should I play it?
output: Yes, play Polluted Delta, but understand it enters as a Mountain under Blood Moon. You cannot activate its fetchland ability because Blood Moon removes all abilities and replaces the land type with Mountain. However, it still taps for R (red mana). If you need red mana, this is fine. If you need to search for an Island to cast Force of Will, you must destroy Blood Moon first. Consider: do you have a way to remove Blood Moon (Force of Vigor, Abrupt Decay)? If so, play the Delta now and activate it after Moon is gone.

instruction: Board state: I have 2 Islands and a Daze in hand. Opponent casts Show and Tell with 1 Island untapped. Should I Daze?
output: Yes, Daze the Show and Tell. Show and Tell costs 2U (3 mana). Your opponent has enough mana to cast it but only 1 Island untapped. If you cast Daze (returning your Island to hand), they must pay 1 to avoid the counter. They have 1 untapped Island, so they CAN pay. BUT - look at the bigger picture. If they pay for Daze, they have used their Show and Tell and 1 extra mana. You still have 1 Island in play. If they cannot pay (maybe the untapped land is not an Island), the Show and Tell is countered. Daze here forces them to either lose their combo or use their remaining mana. Either way, you bought time.

instruction: Board state: My opponent has Leyline of the Void in play (started in opening hand). I am playing Doomsday. Can I still win?
output: This is very difficult but not impossible. Leyline means your cards go to exile instead of the graveyard. This means: you cannot use graveyard-based Doomsday piles. However, Thassa's Oracle wins by checking devotion to blue vs cards in library, not the graveyard. Your pile needs to draw through itself WITHOUT putting cards in the graveyard. Pile example: Brainstorm, Ponder, Thought Scour (mills go to exile, fine), Thassa's Oracle, Force of Will (protection). You need to cast Doomsday, then draw through with cantrips and cast Oracle with 0 cards in library. The key is that Oracle checks library size, not graveyard - Leyline does not stop the win condition itself.

instruction: Board state: I control Painter's Servant (naming blue) and Goblin Welder. Opponent destroys my Grindstone with Force of Vigor. What do I do?
output: Goblin Welder to the rescue. In response to Force of Vigor (or after it resolves if Grindstone is already in the graveyard), activate Goblin Welder: swap Grindstone from the graveyard with any artifact on the battlefield (you can sacrifice a Lotus Petal or other cheap artifact). Grindstone returns to the battlefield untapped. You can then activate it immediately to combo off. This is exactly why Painter plays Goblin Welder - it provides instant-speed recursion that makes killing combo pieces very difficult. The opponent needs to kill Welder AND the combo piece to truly stop you.

instruction: Board state: I am at 3 life. Opponent has Orcish Bowmasters (1/1) and an Orc Army token (3/3). I have Toxic Deluge in hand. What X should I choose?
output: You are at 3 life and Toxic Deluge costs X life to give all creatures -X/-X until end of turn. You need X=3 to kill the 3/3 Army token (and the 1/1 Bowmasters dies too at X=1). But paying 3 life at 3 life kills you. X=1 kills only Bowmasters (1/1 gets -1/-1 = dies) but leaves the Army as a 2/2. X=2 kills Bowmasters and makes the Army a 1/1. You cannot afford X=3. Best play: X=2 (costs 2 life, you go to 1). Bowmasters dies, Army becomes 1/1 until end of turn. You survive but are at 1 life. If you have any other removal, use X=1 and deal with the Army separately.

instruction: Board state: Opponent plays Chalice of the Void. I have Force of Will and a blue card in hand. Chalice is being cast with X=1 (2 charge counters, total cost 2). Can I Force it?
output: Yes, absolutely. Chalice of the Void with X=1 has mana value 2 on the stack (X=1, XX=2). It does not counter itself or Force of Will. Force of Will has mana value 5 (even when pitched for free). Chalice only counters spells with the specified mana value AFTER it resolves and is on the battlefield. While Chalice is on the stack, it is just a spell being cast. Force of Will it now before it resolves. If you let Chalice resolve, then your 1-mana spells (Brainstorm, Ponder, Swords) will be countered, but Force of Will (MV 5) will still work fine.

instruction: Board state: I have lethal on board (Murktide 8/8, opponent at 8 life). Opponent is tapped out. Should I attack or hold up Daze?
output: Attack for lethal. Your opponent is tapped out, meaning they cannot cast any spells (no mana for removal, no alternative costs except pitch spells like Force of Will or Solitude). If they have Force of Will, they could theoretically counter your... wait, Force counters spells not attacks. If they have Solitude, they could exile your Murktide by pitching a white card. If you suspect Solitude, holding Daze does nothing since Daze counters spells and Solitude's evoke is an alternative casting cost, which Daze could counter. Actually - attack. If they Solitude your Murktide, you can Daze the Solitude (they are tapped out and cannot pay 1). Attack with Daze backup is the correct line.

instruction: Board state: It is my opponent's turn. They have Emrakul, the Aeons Torn in play via Sneak Attack (it will be sacrificed at end of turn). They declare attack. Emrakul has annihilator 6. I have 7 permanents. What do I sacrifice?
output: You must sacrifice 6 of your 7 permanents to annihilator 6. You choose which 6. Strategic priority for keeping: 1) Keep the permanent that gives you the best chance to win next turn (if you survive). 2) Keep a land if you need mana to cast a response. 3) Keep a creature if it can trade with something relevant. If Emrakul deals 15 damage and you survive, the Emrakul from Sneak Attack is sacrificed at end of turn. You get ONE turn to rebuild. Keep your best single permanent. In practice, getting annihilated for 6 usually means you lose, but if you are above 15 life, you might survive the combat and steal a win. Note: Emrakul's controller also gets an extra turn (cast trigger) if it was cast, but Sneak Attack puts it into play without casting, so NO extra turn.

instruction: I have Brainstorm and a fetchland. What is the correct sequencing?
output:
The classic Brainstorm sequence:

1) Do NOT crack the fetchland first (you lose the shuffle).
2) Cast Brainstorm. Draw 3 cards.
3) Look at your hand. Identify the 2 worst cards.
4) Put the 2 worst cards on top of your library.
5) NOW crack the fetchland. Search and shuffle.
6) The 2 bad cards are shuffled into your deck.

Net result: You drew the best of 4 cards (3 from Brainstorm + 1 from the fresh draw later) and permanently removed 2 bad cards from the top of your deck.

Common mistake: cracking the fetchland before Brainstorm. This wastes the shuffle. Another mistake: putting back cards you might need - always consider what your opponent might be doing before choosing the 2 to put back.

instruction: I am playing TES. I have Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, Burning Wish, and 1 land in play. Walk me through the kill.
output:
Here is the sequence:

1) Cast Dark Ritual (BBB in pool). Storm count: 1.
2) Cast Lion's Eye Diamond (resolves, now on battlefield). Storm count: 2.
3) Cast Burning Wish. Hold priority BEFORE Wish resolves.
4) Crack LED in response to Burning Wish (LED's ability is a mana ability, does not use stack). Discard your hand (empty anyway). Add BBB. Pool: BBB from Ritual + BBB from LED = BBBBBB.
5) Burning Wish resolves. Search sideboard for Tendrils of Agony.
6) Cast Tendrils of Agony from your hand. Storm count: 4 (Ritual, LED, Wish, Tendrils). Tendrils creates 4 copies. Each copy drains 2 life.
7) Total drain: 5 Tendrils (original + 4 copies) x 2 = 10 life.

That is only 10 damage, not lethal from 20. You need more storm count. If you had an additional ritual or cantrip earlier in the turn, each additional spell adds another Tendrils copy (+2 damage). The typical TES kill needs storm count 9+ for lethal.

instruction: I am playing Lands. I have Life from the Loam in graveyard, Exploration in play, and Wasteland. How do I lock my opponent out?
output:
The Wasteland lock sequence:

1) During your draw step, choose to dredge Life from the Loam instead of drawing (dredge 3 - mill 3 cards from library to graveyard, return Loam to hand).
2) Cast Life from the Loam returning Wasteland and 2 other lands from your graveyard to your hand.
3) With Exploration, you can play 2 lands per turn. Play Wasteland. Play another land.
4) Activate Wasteland targeting opponent's best land. Destroy it.
5) Next turn, dredge Loam again, return Wasteland to hand, repeat.

This creates a recurring loop: every turn you destroy one of their lands while never running out of your own. Combined with Rishadan Port to tap another land, your opponent effectively has 0 usable lands per turn. This is the Lands deck's primary control strategy and is extremely difficult to break once established.

instruction: Board state: I have Stoneforge Mystic in play with Batterskull in hand. Opponent has Kolaghan's Command in hand (I know from Thoughtseize). Should I activate Stoneforge?
output: Yes, activate Stoneforge to put Batterskull into play now. Kolaghan's Command can destroy an artifact AND force a discard. If you wait and keep Batterskull in hand, K-Command can make you discard Batterskull AND destroy something else. If you put Batterskull into play, K-Command can still destroy it, but at least it does not also strip another card from your hand. Worst case: they K-Command destroying Batterskull + something else, and Batterskull goes to graveyard where you can return it to hand (Batterskull has a bounce ability). By playing it now, you get at least one attack/block and force them to use K-Command defensively.

instruction: Board state: I am playing D&T. I have Aether Vial on 2 and Flickerwisp in hand. Opponent attacks with Murktide Regent (7/7). How do I handle this?
output: You cannot Vial in Flickerwisp because it costs 3, not 2 (Vial has 2 counters). If you had Vial on 3, you could Flickerwisp the Murktide before damage to exile it until end step. With Vial on 2, check what 2-cost creatures you have in hand: Stoneforge Mystic (tutor Batterskull to block next turn), Thalia (does not help here), Phyrexian Revoker (name Murktide? No, it does not have activated abilities). Best play: take the 7 damage, tick Vial to 3, and next attack you can Flickerwisp the Murktide. Alternatively, if you have Swords to Plowshares, cast it targeting Murktide.

instruction: Board state: I am at 4 life playing Reanimator. I have Reanimate and Griselbrand in graveyard. Should I Reanimate it?
output: Reanimate Griselbrand costs life equal to its mana value: 8. You are at 4 life. Reanimating Griselbrand puts you to -4 life and you die before gaining any value. DO NOT Reanimate Griselbrand here. Your options: 1) Reanimate a cheaper creature if one exists in a graveyard (Archon of Cruelty costs 8 too, so same problem). 2) Use Animate Dead or Exhume instead (they do not cost life). 3) Find a way to gain life first. 4) Wait until your life total can sustain the Reanimate cost. Life management is critical in Reanimator.

instruction: Board state: My opponent cast Thoughtseize. My hand is: Force of Will, Brainstorm, Ponder, Murktide Regent, Underground Sea. What do they take?
output:
From the opponent's perspective, they are choosing what hurts you most. Force of Will is the correct take in most scenarios because: 1) It is your only free interaction - without it, they can combo or deploy threats freely. 2) Brainstorm and Ponder will find more cards, but Force is unique. 3) Murktide is a threat but can be answered by removal later.

However, if the opponent is on combo (Storm, Show and Tell), they take Force 100% of the time. If they are on fair blue, they might take Murktide since it is your best threat and they plan to grind. If they are on aggro, they might take Murktide to prevent being raced.

As the player being Thoughtseized: after they take Force, immediately Brainstorm to hide your best remaining card(s) on top of your library where Thoughtseize cannot touch them. This is a key response pattern.

instruction: Board state: I have Thalia Guardian of Thraben in play. Opponent has 3 mana available. Can they cast Brainstorm + Ponder this turn?
output: With Thalia in play, noncreature spells cost 1 more. Brainstorm normally costs U, now costs 1U (2 mana). Ponder normally costs U, now costs 1U (2 mana). With 3 mana, the opponent can cast either Brainstorm OR Ponder, but not both (2 + 2 = 4 mana needed). Thalia effectively cuts their cantrip output in half. This is why Thalia is so strong against cantrip decks: they can no longer efficiently chain cantrips to find answers.

instruction: Board state: Opponent has Ancient Tomb, City of Traitors, and Chalice of the Void on 1. They play a second Chalice on 0. What does this lock out?
output: Chalice on 1 counters all spells with mana value 1: Brainstorm, Ponder, Swords to Plowshares, Lightning Bolt, Thoughtseize, Daze, Delver, and most 1-mana interaction. Chalice on 0 counters all spells with mana value 0: Lotus Petal, Mox Opal, Mishra's Bauble, Engineered Explosives (cast for X=0), Chrome Mox, and Mox Diamond. Together, they lock out everything costing 0 or 1. Your opponent still has access to 2+ mana spells: Force of Will (5), Show and Tell (3), Murktide Regent (2 with delve). Focus your game plan on 2+ mana spells and use Force of Vigor (free by pitching green) to destroy the Chalices.

instruction: Board state: I am playing TES. I have 5 mana, Ad Nauseam in hand, and I am at 14 life. Is it safe to cast Ad Nauseam?
output: Ad Nauseam reveals cards from the top of your library and puts them into your hand, losing life equal to each card's mana value. You stop whenever you choose. At 14 life, you can safely reveal approximately 10-14 cards (average mana value in TES is about 1.0-1.2). This should give you enough rituals, LED, and Burning Wish to kill. Key risks: revealing expensive spells back-to-back (Burning Wish at MV 2, Force of Will at MV 5 could cost 7 life in 2 reveals). Stop when you have enough mana + storm for lethal Tendrils. Safe threshold: stop when you are at 4-5 life remaining to avoid dying to your own Storm next turn. 14 life is a comfortable Ad Nauseam.

instruction: Board state: I have Dark Depths with 0 ice counters and Thespian's Stage. Opponent has Wasteland untapped. What is my line?
output:
If you activate Thespian's Stage to copy Dark Depths while opponent has Wasteland untapped, they can respond to the activation by Wastelanding either Dark Depths or Thespian's Stage. If they Wasteland the Stage, the copy ability fizzles and you have no Stage and still a Depths with 0 counters. If they Wasteland Dark Depths, the copy ability still resolves (it was already on the stack) and Stage becomes a copy of Dark Depths with 0 counters, creating Marit Lage.

Best line: activate Stage targeting Dark Depths. If they Wasteland Depths in response, you still get Marit Lage (Stage copies the last known state). If they Wasteland Stage, you lose but can recover with Crop Rotation or Life from the Loam. If they Wasteland before you activate, you lost your window. Act during their end step to minimize their response window.

instruction: Board state: Opponent has True-Name Nemesis (protection from me). I need to remove it. What are my options?
output:
True-Name Nemesis has protection from you, which means you cannot target it, deal damage to it, block it, or enchant it. Options that work:

1) Non-targeting removal: Toxic Deluge (-X/-X to all creatures, does not target), Terminus (tuck all creatures, does not target), Council's Judgment (opponents vote, does not target), Supreme Verdict (destroy all, does not target).

2) Sacrifice effects: Liliana of the Veil -2 (opponent sacrifices), Diabolic Edict (opponent sacrifices), Sheoldred's Edict.

3) Mass bounce: Cyclonic Rift overloaded (does not target when overloaded).

4) Race it: TNN is a 3/1, dealing 3 per turn. If you have a faster clock, just race and ignore it.

The most played answer is Toxic Deluge (13% of decks) since it handles TNN and anything else for variable cost. Terminus is next best in UW decks.

instruction: Board state: I have Brainstorm and 2 fetchlands in play, no other cards in hand. Should I crack a fetch first or Brainstorm first?
output:
Brainstorm first, then crack fetch. Here is why:

1) Cast Brainstorm: draw 3 cards, put 2 bad ones on top.
2) Crack fetchland: shuffle away the 2 bad cards.
3) You kept the best of 4 cards (3 drawn + eventual draw) and have 1 fetchland remaining for the next Brainstorm.

If you crack fetch first, you waste a shuffle effect. Then after Brainstorm, you put 2 bad cards on top and only have 1 fetch left to shuffle (instead of 2). You would draw 1 bad card before shuffling the second away.

Exception: if you need a specific color of mana RIGHT NOW to cast Brainstorm (e.g., you need U and only have a fetchland), then fetch first to get an Island, then Brainstorm. But if you already have U available, always Brainstorm first.

instruction: Board state: I control Blood Moon. Opponent plays Boseiju, Who Endures (channel ability from hand). Can they destroy Blood Moon?
output: Yes. Boseiju's channel ability is activated from the hand, not from the battlefield. Blood Moon only affects nonbasic lands ON THE BATTLEFIELD. Boseiju's channel says 'discard this card: destroy target artifact, enchantment, or nonbasic land an opponent controls.' This is a hand ability, not a land ability, so Blood Moon does not interfere. The opponent discards Boseiju from hand and destroys Blood Moon. This is one of the few ways to answer Blood Moon from under Blood Moon.

instruction: Board state: My opponent controls Omniscience. They start casting spells for free. Can I do anything?
output: Omniscience lets them cast spells for free, but each spell still goes on the stack and you get priority to respond. You can: 1) Force of Will their most important spell. 2) Flusterstorm their key instant or sorcery (storm copies counter all the things). 3) Stifle a critical triggered ability. 4) If you have Karakas, bounce their legendary creature after it enters. 5) If you have your own Show and Tell in hand and Omniscience puts in a creature, Show and Tell your own threat. The key: even with Omniscience, they still use the stack. You are not helpless if you held up interaction.

instruction: Board state: I have 2 creatures. Opponent casts Toxic Deluge paying X=3. In response, can I save my creatures with Ephemerate?
output: Yes, partially. Ephemerate exiles one creature you control and returns it at the next end step. Cast Ephemerate targeting your best creature in response to Toxic Deluge. Your creature is exiled (safe from Deluge). Toxic Deluge resolves, giving all creatures -3/-3 - your other creature dies if it has 3 or less toughness. At the next end step, your Ephemerated creature returns to the battlefield fresh. You saved one creature from a board wipe. Note: Ephemerate has rebound, so next upkeep you can do it again for free.

instruction: Board state: Opponent has Thalia (noncreature spells cost 1 more) and Spirit of the Labyrinth (each player may draw only 1 card per turn). How do I fight through this?
output: This is a devastating D&T lock. Your cantrips cost 2 instead of 1, and drawing extra cards is impossible (Brainstorm draws only 1). Priorities: 1) Kill Spirit of the Labyrinth first - it prevents cantripping entirely. Use a removal spell (Swords for W+1, Bolt for R+1, Push for B+1 with Thalia tax). 2) Then kill Thalia to restore normal spell costs. 3) If you cannot remove either, deploy a threat that does not require cantrips (flash in Bowmasters) and try to race. 4) Toxic Deluge at 1BB+1 (Thalia tax) = 2BB clears both. This is the cleanest answer but costs 4 mana + life.

instruction: Board state: I have Show and Tell in hand but opponent has Containment Priest in hand (I know from Gitaxian Probe). Should I still cast Show and Tell?
output:
No, or at least not without a plan. If you cast Show and Tell, opponent puts Containment Priest into play. Priest says: if a nontoken creature would enter the battlefield and it was not cast, exile it. Your Emrakul/Atraxa enters via Show and Tell (not cast), so it gets exiled. You put in a 15-mana creature, they exile it for free.

Better lines: 1) Use Sneak Attack instead (you can respond to Priest's ETB by Sneaking in Emrakul before Priest settles). Wait, Priest's replacement effect applies immediately. 2) Counter or remove Priest first. 3) Put in a non-creature permanent (Omniscience, Sneak Attack enchantment) since Priest only stops creatures. 4) Wait until they use Priest for another purpose or side it out.

instruction: Board state: I am at 1 life playing Storm. I need to win this turn or die. I have Dark Ritual, LED, Burning Wish, Brainstorm, and 1 Swamp.
output: You can win! Sequence: 1) Swamp, cast Dark Ritual (BBB in pool, storm 1). 2) Cast Brainstorm (draw 3, put 2 back, storm 2). This finds more gas if available. 3) Cast Lion's Eye Diamond (0 mana, storm 3). 4) Cast Burning Wish, hold priority. 5) Crack LED for BBB in response (discard hand, pool now BBB + whatever remains from Ritual, storm still 3). 6) Burning Wish resolves, get Tendrils of Agony from sideboard. 7) Cast Tendrils (storm 4 now, Tendrils makes 4 copies = 5 total = 10 drain). 8) Wait - that is only 10 damage if opponent is at 20. You need storm 9 for lethal. With only these 5 cards, storm count is 5 (Ritual, Brainstorm, LED, Wish, Tendrils = 5 spells). Tendrils creates 5 copies + original = 12 life drain total. If opponent is at 12 or less, you win!

instruction: Board state: I am playing Dimir Tempo. I have Murktide Regent (6/6) and opponent is at 6 life with Swords to Plowshares in hand (I know from Thoughtseize). Should I attack?
output: If you attack, opponent Swords your Murktide, exiling it and gaining 6 life (goes to 12). You lose your threat and they stabilize. If you do NOT attack, you hold Murktide as a blocker and threat while developing your board further. Better line: deploy another threat first (Bowmasters, Barrowgoyf) to force them to choose which to Swords. Then attack with the surviving threat. If you only have Murktide, consider: is the opponent deploying their own threats? If they have nothing, wait. If they are building a board, you may need to force the Swords now and rely on future topdecks. Context matters, but usually do not walk Murktide into known Swords without a backup plan.

instruction: Board state: It is game 2 against Oops All Spells. I mulliganed to 6 and have Leyline of the Void, Force of Will, Brainstorm, Ponder, Island, Polluted Delta. Should I keep?
output: Absolutely snap keep. This is the dream hand against Oops: Leyline of the Void starts in play (completely shuts off their combo), Force of Will protects the Leyline from Chain of Vapor or bounce effects, and Brainstorm + Ponder find you a threat to kill them with. With Leyline in play, they literally cannot win through their normal gameplan. They need to find artifact/enchantment removal for Leyline, and you have Force to stop that. Island + Polluted Delta provides mana. This 6-card hand is better than most 7-card hands. 10/10.

instruction: Board state: Opponent casts Natural Order sacrificing a mana elf, searching for Craterhoof Behemoth. They have 5 other creatures in play. Can I survive?
output: Craterhoof Behemoth enters and gives all their creatures +X/+X and trample where X is the number of creatures they control. With 5 creatures + Craterhoof = 6 creatures, everything gets +6/+6 and trample. A typical Elves board of five 1/1 elves becomes five 7/7 tramplers plus a 11/11 Craterhoof = 46 damage with trample. You probably cannot survive this through blocking. Your options: 1) Counter Natural Order with Force of Will. 2) Kill the sacrifice target in response (but they already sacrificed as a cost). 3) Swords to Plowshares Craterhoof before the trigger resolves... wait, the +X/+X is an ETB trigger that goes on the stack. You CAN respond by removing Craterhoof, which means X = 5 instead of 6. Still likely lethal. Your best bet was countering Natural Order.

instruction: Board state: I am playing Storm (TES). My opponent has Thalia, Guardian of Thraben in play. How much extra mana do I need to combo?
output: Thalia adds 1 to every noncreature spell. Your typical combo sequence costs: Dark Ritual (B+1=1B), Rite of Flame (R+1=1R), Lion's Eye Diamond (0+1=1), Burning Wish (1R+1=2R), Tendrils of Agony (2BB+1=3BB). Each spell costs 1 more. If you cast 5 spells, that is 5 extra mana needed. A normal TES kill needs about 4-5 mana to start; with Thalia, you need 9-10 mana. This is very difficult but not impossible. Lines: 1) Use Burning Wish for Pulverize (destroy all artifacts, but Thalia is a creature, not an artifact). 2) Use Grapeshot or Lightning Bolt effect to kill Thalia first. 3) Generate enough extra mana through additional rituals. The best play is usually to kill Thalia with your first spell (Abrupt Decay costs 1BG+1=2BG with Thalia) then combo normally.

instruction: Board state: Both players have Orcish Bowmasters in play. I want to cast Brainstorm. What happens?
output: When you cast Brainstorm, you draw 3 cards. Your opponent's Bowmasters triggers for each card you draw beyond the first draw of the turn. If this is your first draw action (before draw step or you are in your draw step), the first Brainstorm draw is normal, draws 2 and 3 trigger opponent's Bowmasters twice. If you already drew for turn, all 3 Brainstorm draws trigger (3 times). Your OWN Bowmasters does NOT trigger because it only triggers on OPPONENT's draws, not yours. So: opponent gets 2-3 triggers (2-3 damage to you or your creatures, Army grows by 2-3). This is why Brainstorm timing against Bowmasters is critical - sometimes it is correct to NOT cantrip into opposing Bowmasters.

instruction: Board state: I have Chalice of the Void on 1 and my opponent just cast Abrupt Decay targeting it. Can I counter Abrupt Decay?
output: No. Abrupt Decay has 'this spell can't be countered' which means no counter effect works against it. Additionally, even though Abrupt Decay has mana value 2 (not 1), so your Chalice on 1 would not have countered it anyway. Your Chalice is going to be destroyed. The only ways to protect against Abrupt Decay are: 1) prevent it from being cast (Thoughtseize it, Thalia taxes it), 2) give your permanent hexproof or shroud (Sterling Grove protects enchantments), 3) sacrifice the targeted permanent in response for value. Against Abrupt Decay specifically, proactive disruption is the only reliable answer.

instruction: Board state: My opponent has Solitary Confinement in play (prevent all damage, skip draw step, shroud). I cannot target them or deal damage. How do I win?
output: Solitary Confinement prevents damage and gives shroud (cannot be targeted), but it requires discarding a card at upkeep and the controller skips their draw step. Options: 1) Wait. If they cannot draw cards, they will eventually run out of cards to discard and Confinement is sacrificed. Enchantress draws cards from enchantments to sustain it, so you need to disrupt their draw engine. 2) Destroy Confinement: it has shroud so targeted removal fails. Use non-targeting removal: Engineered Explosives on 3, Oblivion Stone, Council's Judgment, or Cryptic Command's bounce mode. 3) Force them to sacrifice: Liliana of the Veil's -2 makes them sacrifice a permanent (you choose which eventually empties their board). 4) Win without damage: Tendrils of Agony causes LOSS OF LIFE, not damage. Confinement prevents damage but not life loss. Storm can win through Confinement!

instruction: Board state: I control a flipped Delver (3/2 flyer). Opponent controls Orcish Bowmasters (1/1) and Orc Army (2/2). I have Lightning Bolt in hand. What is the best attack/removal sequence?
output:
Key decision: what to Bolt. Option A: Bolt Bowmasters (kills the 1/1). Army stays as 2/2. Attack with Delver for 3 (opponent goes to -3 if they block with Army and Delver dies, or takes 3 if they do not block). Option B: Bolt the Army (2 damage does not kill a 2/2... wait, Army has 2 toughness, Bolt does 3, Army dies). Army dies, Bowmasters stays as 1/1. Attack with Delver for 3 (opponent blocks with 1/1 Bowmasters, your 3/2 kills their 1/1, you take 0). Option C: Bolt opponent's face for 3, attack for 3 = 6 damage total but their board stays.

Best line: Bolt Bowmasters first (kill the value engine that triggers on every cantrip). Then attack with Delver into the 2/2 Army - they can block (trade: Delver dies, Army dies) or take 3. Killing Bowmasters is priority #1 because it generates value every turn you cantrip.

instruction: Board state: I am playing Lands. I have Exploration, 3 lands in play, and Life from the Loam plus Wasteland plus Ghost Quarter in hand. Opponent has 2 nonbasic lands. Can I destroy both this turn?
output: Yes! With Exploration, you can play 2 lands per turn. Sequence: 1) Play Wasteland from hand (land drop 1 of 2 via Exploration). 2) Activate Wasteland, sacrificing it, destroying opponent's first nonbasic. 3) Play Ghost Quarter from hand (land drop 2 of 2). 4) Activate Ghost Quarter, sacrificing it, destroying opponent's second nonbasic (they search for a basic). You destroyed both nonbasics. Ghost Quarter gives them a basic, but Wasteland does not. Next turn, dredge Life from the Loam to return Wasteland + Ghost Quarter + another land to hand, and do it again. With Exploration, you destroy 2 lands per turn indefinitely.

instruction: Board state: Both players are in topdeck mode (no cards in hand). I have 3 lands. Opponent has a Jace the Mind Sculptor at 6 loyalty. What am I hoping to draw?
output: You need to kill Jace immediately or you lose to the +2 every turn (opponent controls your draws). Best topdecks: 1) Orcish Bowmasters (flash in, ping Jace for 1, attack for 1 next turn = slow but possible). 2) Lightning Bolt (3 damage to Jace, goes to 3 loyalty, need another bolt). 3) Murktide Regent (if graveyard has 5+ cards, cast for UU, attack Jace for 3+, need another attack). 4) Council's Judgment (exiles Jace for 1WW). 5) Abrupt Decay (destroys permanent MV 3 or less... Jace is MV 4, cannot be Decayed). 6) Wasteland (does not help vs Jace). 7) Any creature with 7+ power to kill Jace in one attack (rare in topdeck mode). You are in a very bad position. Jace in topdeck mode is nearly game over because the +2 controls what you draw.

instruction: Board state: I have a resolved Aluren in play (can cast creatures MV 3 or less for free). I have Acererak the Archlich in hand. Walk me through the kill.
output: With Aluren in play, cast Acererak (MV 3) for free. Acererak's ETB triggers: you may venture into a dungeon. Choose Lost Mine of Phandelver. Go to room 1 (gain 1 life... actually you want to NOT complete the dungeon). Re-read: Acererak says 'when Acererak enters, if you have NOT completed a dungeon, return Acererak to its owner's hand.' Since you have not completed a dungeon yet, Acererak bounces to hand. Cast Acererak again (free with Aluren). Again, you have not completed the dungeon, so it bounces. But wait - each entry DOES progress the dungeon. After entering enough times, you will complete Lost Mine. The last room deals 1 damage to each opponent. But you need to loop through multiple dungeon completions for lethal. Each complete dungeon run = 1 damage + other effects. You loop until opponent is dead. It takes about 20 loops for 20 damage.

instruction: Board state: I have Helm of Obedience and Rest in Peace in play. How does the combo work?
output: Helm of Obedience costs X and taps: opponent puts cards from top of library into graveyard until a creature card or X cards are put there, then you gain control of that creature. With Rest in Peace in play, cards that would go to the graveyard are exiled instead. Activate Helm for X=1. Opponent reveals the top card: it would go to the graveyard, but Rest in Peace exiles it instead. Since it was exiled and NOT put into the graveyard, the condition 'until a creature card or X cards are put into the graveyard' is never satisfied. The ability continues indefinitely, exiling the opponent's ENTIRE library. They lose on their next draw step. This combo wins at instant speed for just 1 mana activation cost (X=1 plus tapping Helm).

instruction: Board state: My opponent plays Terminus for its miracle cost (W). Can I save my creatures?
output: Terminus is a sorcery that puts all creatures on the bottom of their owners' libraries. When miracle is revealed, the trigger goes on the stack. You can respond to the miracle trigger (before they cast Terminus) or to the spell itself (after they cast it for W). Your options: 1) Bounce your own creatures to hand (Karakas for legends, Ephemerate exiles and returns later). 2) Sacrifice your creatures for value before Terminus resolves (to Goblin Bombardment, Cabal Therapy flashback, etc.). 3) Counter Terminus (Force of Will, Daze - but it only costs W so Daze is less effective). 4) Stifle the miracle trigger (they cannot cast it for W, must pay full 4WW). Note: Terminus puts creatures on the BOTTOM of libraries, not the graveyard, so sacrifice is the only way to get death/graveyard triggers.

instruction: Board state: I have Food Chain in play and Misthollow Griffin in hand. Walk me through generating infinite mana.
output:
Food Chain: exile a creature you control, add mana equal to that creature's mana value +1 (only for creature spells). Misthollow Griffin (MV 4, flying 3/3) can be cast from exile.

1) Cast Misthollow Griffin from hand for 3U (4 mana).
2) Exile Griffin to Food Chain: get 5 mana (MV 4 + 1) of any one color, usable only for creature spells.
3) Cast Griffin from exile for 3U (paying 4 of the 5 mana). Net: +1 mana.
4) Exile Griffin again: get 5 mana. Cast Griffin: spend 4. Net: +1 more mana.
5) Repeat infinitely. Each loop nets +1 mana for creature spells.

With infinite creature mana, cast Walking Ballista for X=infinite. Ballista enters with infinite +1/+1 counters. Remove counters to deal 1 damage each to opponent for infinite damage. Alternative: cast Thassa's Oracle with infinite devotion to blue for the win.

instruction: Board state: Opponent at 20 life. I have 7 goblins in play including Goblin Ringleader and Muxus. I just drew Goblin Lackey. Can I kill them?
output:
You have 7 goblins for a total attack of approximately 7-12 power depending on lords and specific creatures. That is not lethal from 20 in one swing unless you have Goblin Chieftain (+1/+1 lord) or multiple lords. However, Goblin Lackey has a special ability: whenever it deals combat damage to a player, put a Goblin from your hand into play. If Lackey connects (1 damage), you could put a powerful goblin into play for free. But you just drew Lackey, so it has summoning sickness and cannot attack this turn.

Better question: can you attack with your 7 goblins for enough damage? 7 creatures with average power 1-2 = 7-14 damage. Not lethal from 20. You need another turn or a pump effect. If one of your goblins is Goblin Chieftain (lord giving +1/+1 and haste), your 7 goblins attack for ~14-21 damage. With 2 lords, easily lethal. Without lords, you need 2 combat steps.

instruction: Board state: I have 3 mana and Swords to Plowshares in hand. Opponent attacks with Emrakul, the Aeons Torn (protection from colored spells) via Sneak Attack. Can I do anything?
output: Swords cannot target Emrakul (protection from colored spells). Your options: 1) If you have Karakas, tap it to bounce Emrakul (land ability, not a spell). 2) Sacrifice permanents to annihilator 6 (you lose 6 permanents but survive if you have more than 15 life). 3) Fog effects (Darkness costs 1B, prevents all combat damage this turn). 4) Flash in a creature that can block (but Emrakul has flying, so you need a flyer or reach creature). 5) Maze of Ith untaps and removes from combat (land ability, not a colored spell). Without Karakas or Maze, you are likely dead to 15 damage + annihilator 6.

instruction: Board state: I have Natural Order in hand and 4 elves in play including Heritage Druid. Opponent has Force of Will and a blue card (I know from Thoughtseize). How do I play this?
output: You need to either bait or play around Force. Options: 1) Cast a lesser threat first (Elvish Visionary, etc.) to bait Force, then Natural Order next turn. Risk: they save Force for NO. 2) Just cast Natural Order into Force. You lose NO and a sacrificed elf (2-for-2 plus Force's pitch = 3 cards from opponent). This leaves you with 3 elves and another draw step to find a second NO or GSZ. 3) If you have enough mana from Heritage Druid, cast multiple spells to force them to use Force on the wrong one. Heritage Druid taps 3 elves for GGG. Use GGG to cast NO (costs 2GG, need one more G from a land). If they Force, you still have elves and mana for Craterhoof via another path. Line 3 is best: deploy a threat THEN Natural Order. They must Force one; the other resolves.

instruction: Board state: I am playing Doomsday. I just resolved Doomsday at 7 life. I have Ponder in hand and 1 Island untapped. Construct a pile.
output:
Available resources: Ponder in hand, 1 blue mana, 7 life (Doomsday cost half your life rounded up from starting life). Pile (top to bottom): 1) Brainstorm 2) LED (Lion's Eye Diamond) 3) Thassa's Oracle 4) Force of Will 5) A blue card (for Force pitch)

Sequence: Cast Ponder (U), see top 3: Brainstorm, LED, Oracle. Take Brainstorm (draw it). Island untapped. Need more mana. Cast Brainstorm (U from tapping Island): draw LED, Oracle, Force of Will. Put back 2 cards (does not matter, library has 2 left). Play LED (0). Cast Oracle... wait, Oracle costs UU. You have 0 mana and LED gives BBB or UUU. Crack LED for UUU, discarding hand (Force goes to graveyard). Cast Oracle for UU (1 mana remaining). Oracle ETB: devotion to blue = 2 (UU in Oracle's cost). Cards in library = 2. 2 >= 2 = you win!

Wait - you need to check: after drawing with Brainstorm and putting 2 back, library has 2 cards. Oracle checks on ETB. Devotion to blue = Oracle (UU in its mana cost) = 2. Library = 2. 2 >= 2. You win!

instruction: Board state: I have Batterskull equipped to a Germ token (4/4 vigilance lifelink). Opponent casts Kolaghan's Command choosing 'destroy an artifact' and 'deal 2 damage to any target.' What happens?
output: Kolaghan's Command's two modes resolve in order: 1) Destroy target artifact: destroys Batterskull (it is an artifact). 2) Deal 2 damage to target: deals 2 to the Germ (or you, or another target). If Batterskull is destroyed, the Germ token is still a 0/0 creature (base stats with no equipment). It dies to state-based actions (0 toughness) before the 2 damage mode even matters. If opponent chose to deal 2 damage to you instead, Batterskull is still destroyed and the Germ dies as a 0/0. However - Batterskull has a return ability: pay 3 mana to return it to hand. If you have 3 mana, you can activate this in response to K-Command, saving Batterskull by bouncing it to hand (Germ dies either way since it becomes 0/0 when Batterskull leaves).

instruction: Board state: My opponent controls Narset, Parter of Veils (each opponent can draw only 1 card per turn). I have Sylvan Library in play. During my draw step, what happens?
output: Your draw step: you draw your normal card for the turn (1 draw). Then Sylvan Library triggers: you may draw 2 additional cards. However, Narset prevents you from drawing more than 1 card per turn. Your normal draw already used your 1 allowed draw. Sylvan Library's 2 extra draws are replaced by nothing (you simply cannot draw). You drew 1 card total, and Sylvan Library's 'put 2 back or pay 8 life' still applies. Since you only drew 1 card this turn, you can choose to put 0 back and pay 0 life, or put 1 back (the card you drew). Narset completely shuts off Sylvan Library's advantage. To fix this: remove Narset first, then Library becomes a draw engine again.

instruction: Board state: I have Painter's Servant naming blue in play. Opponent casts Veil of Summer. What happens?
output: Veil of Summer says: you and spells you control cannot be the target of blue or black spells or abilities this turn. Draw a card if an opponent has cast a blue or black spell this turn. With Painter naming blue, ALL spells are blue (including your own). When Veil resolves: the opponent draws a card (you have cast blue spells this turn because all your spells are blue). They and their spells gain hexproof from blue and black. This means YOUR Grindstone activation (which targets them) is... wait, Grindstone targets an opponent to mill, and Grindstone is an artifact ability, not a spell. Veil protects from spells and abilities that are blue. The Grindstone ability is now blue (because Painter makes everything blue). Can Veil's protection stop a blue ability? Veil says 'blue or black spells or abilities' so yes. You cannot target the Veil player with Grindstone while Veil is active. Wait until Veil's protection expires at end of turn, then combo.

instruction: Board state: I have 4 Islands and Daze in hand. Opponent casts Show and Tell with 3 mana (2U). They have 1 untapped Island remaining. Should I Daze?
output: Yes, Daze the Show and Tell. Opponent cast S&T for 3 mana and has 1 Island untapped. If you Daze (return your Island to hand), they must pay 1 more to resolve S&T. They have 1 untapped Island, so they CAN pay. But here is the critical question: do they have Force of Will backup? If they pay for Daze, they used all their mana AND their combo spell resolved - they are likely going for the combo this turn. If you do not Daze, S&T resolves for free and they still have 1 untapped Island for... nothing useful. Actually, forcing them to tap their last Island is valuable because it means they cannot activate any instant-speed abilities. Daze here is correct: at minimum it costs them their last mana, and if they cannot pay (maybe the untapped land produces colorless), S&T is countered. Always Daze a must-counter spell when the opponent is mana-constrained.

instruction: Board state: My opponent just cast Terminus miracle for W, putting all creatures on bottom of libraries. I have Karakas and my own Thalia on the battlefield. Can I save Thalia?
output: Yes! In response to Terminus (while it is on the stack), activate Karakas targeting Thalia (she is legendary). Thalia returns to your hand before Terminus resolves. When Terminus resolves, it puts all creatures on the bottom of libraries - but Thalia is safely in your hand, not on the battlefield. Next turn, replay Thalia. You protected your key creature from a board wipe for 0 mana (Karakas tap costs nothing). This is one of the most important Karakas interactions: saving your own legends from removal.

instruction: Board state: I have Leyline of the Void and Helm of Obedience, but opponent has Force of Vigor in hand (I know from Thoughtseize). How do I win?
output:
Force of Vigor can destroy both Leyline and Helm in one shot (it destroys up to 2 artifacts/enchantments). If you activate Helm while both are in play, opponent can Force of Vigor in response: destroy Leyline first. Without Leyline, Helm's activation stops at the first creature or after X cards go to graveyard normally. You do not win.

Better line: activate Helm for X=1 BEFORE they get priority to cast Vigor... wait, they have priority to respond to the activation. The activation goes on the stack, they can Vigor in response. The key: you need to strip the Vigor first. Options: 1) Thoughtseize it (you already know about it). 2) Duress it. 3) Wait until they use Vigor on something else. 4) Force of Will their Vigor in response to Helm activation. If you have Force + blue card, the line is: activate Helm, they Vigor targeting Leyline + Helm, you Force their Vigor. Helm resolves and mills them out.

instruction: Board state: I am playing Elves. I have 5 elves in play, Heritage Druid, and Natural Order in hand but only 2 green mana from lands. Can I cast Natural Order?
output: Yes! Heritage Druid's ability: tap three untapped elves you control to add GGG. You have 5 elves total. Tap 3 (including Heritage Druid itself) for GGG. You now have GGG + 2G from lands = GGGGG (5 green mana). Natural Order costs 2GG (4 mana). Cast Natural Order sacrificing one of your 2 remaining untapped elves (the cost). Search your library for Craterhoof Behemoth. Craterhoof enters: you control 4 creatures (5 original - 1 sacrificed to NO + Craterhoof = 5, wait: 5 - 1 sac'd = 4, + Craterhoof = 5). Craterhoof gives all creatures +5/+5 and trample. Your 4 remaining elves (roughly 1/1 each) become 6/6 tramplers, Craterhoof is 10/10 trample. Total attack: 34 damage with trample. Lethal from 20.

instruction: Board state: I have Life from the Loam in hand and 3 Wastelands in graveyard. Opponent has 3 lands. Can I lock them out this turn?
output: Cast Life from the Loam returning 3 Wastelands to hand. If you have Exploration in play (play 2 lands per turn): play Wasteland #1, activate to destroy opponent's land #1. Play Wasteland #2, activate to destroy land #2. You used both land drops. You cannot play Wasteland #3 this turn. Opponent has 1 land remaining. Next turn: dredge Loam, return Wastelands, repeat. Without Exploration (1 land drop): play 1 Wasteland, destroy 1 land. Opponent has 2 lands. Much slower lock. The Exploration + Loam + Wasteland engine is the heart of Lands' soft lock strategy.

instruction: Board state: Opponent just resolved Omniscience and is casting spells for free. I have Flusterstorm in hand. Is it useful?
output: Yes! Omniscience lets them cast spells for free, but Flusterstorm still works. Flusterstorm counters an instant or sorcery unless its controller pays 1 for EACH copy. Even with Omniscience, paying mana for Flusterstorm copies requires actually having and tapping mana. If opponent is casting everything for free via Omniscience and not tapping lands, they may not have mana available to pay for Flusterstorm copies. However - Omniscience says 'without paying its mana cost' but paying for Flusterstorm is an additional cost, not a mana cost... actually, Flusterstorm says 'pay 1' which is a cost they must pay with actual mana. If they have untapped lands, they can pay. If they used Omniscience for everything and never tapped lands, they might have full mana available. Flusterstorm is still useful but may not be sufficient alone.

instruction: Board state: I control Thalia and Ethersworn Canonist. Opponent wants to cast Brainstorm then Ponder. What happens?
output: Thalia makes noncreature spells cost 1 more. Ethersworn Canonist limits each player to 1 noncreature spell per turn. Opponent wants to cast Brainstorm (normally U, now 1U with Thalia). They can cast Brainstorm for 1U. Ethersworn Canonist then prevents them from casting Ponder (a second noncreature spell). They are limited to 1 noncreature spell per turn. This is the D&T dream: Thalia taxes the one spell they can cast, and Canonist prevents all subsequent spells. With both in play, the opponent can cast exactly 1 noncreature spell per turn at +1 mana cost. Devastating against cantrip-heavy and combo decks.

instruction: Board state: I cast Burning Wish. In response, I crack Lion's Eye Diamond for 3 red mana. My hand is now empty. Burning Wish resolves. What can I get?
output: This is the classic TES play. LED is cracked in response to Burning Wish, discarding your hand (which might have been empty anyway or contained cards you do not need). You gain RRR. Burning Wish resolves: search your sideboard for a sorcery card and put it in your hand. You can get Tendrils of Agony, Empty the Warrens, Past in Flames, or any other sorcery in your sideboard. Then you cast the sorcery using the RRR from LED plus any other mana floating. Tendrils costs 2BB, so you need BB from another source (Dark Ritual mana floating from earlier in the turn). The LED crack in response to Wish is the fundamental TES sequencing trick.

instruction: Board state: My opponent has an empty library and 1 card in hand. I have Surgical Extraction. Can I win?
output: If their 1 card in hand is their last card and their library is empty, they will lose on their next draw step (cannot draw from an empty library = game loss). You do not need to do anything - just pass the turn and they lose. Surgical Extraction is unnecessary here. However, if they might have an instant-speed win (like casting Thassa's Oracle from hand before their draw step), you should consider: can you stop it? If they cast Oracle with 0 cards in library and devotion to blue >= 0, they win. If you have Force of Will, counter Oracle. If not, Surgical in response to Oracle's ETB trigger targeting... Surgical targets a card in graveyard, not a trigger. Surgical cannot stop Oracle. You need a counterspell or Stifle for the trigger.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the play with Sneak and Show: Ancient Tomb, Island, Lotus Petal, Show and Tell, Emrakul the Aeons Torn, Force of Will, Brainstorm?
output: This is a fantastic keep. You can win on turn 2: Island turn 1, then turn 2 Ancient Tomb plus Lotus Petal for Show and Tell putting Emrakul into play. You also have Force of Will to protect against their disruption and Brainstorm to find additional answers if needed. This hand has fast combo, protection, and card selection. Easy 10/10 keep.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Reanimator: Swamp, Dark Ritual, Entomb, Reanimate, Thoughtseize, Grief, Force of Will?
output: This is a snap keep with multiple lines. Turn 1 you can Thoughtseize to clear Force of Will, then Dark Ritual into Entomb plus Reanimate for a turn 1 Griselbrand. Alternatively, evoke Grief to strip their counter, then Entomb-Reanimate. Force of Will protects if they have interaction. This hand has redundant disruption and a fast combo. 10/10.

instruction: It is turn 3 in a Dimir Tempo mirror. I have Murktide Regent in hand, Underground Sea and 2 Islands in play, 6 cards in graveyard. Should I cast Murktide?
output: Yes, deploy Murktide Regent now. With 6 cards in graveyard, you can delve 4-5 to make it at least a 4/4 or 5/5. In the mirror, the first player to deploy a major threat with protection usually wins. Waiting gives your opponent time to find answers. If they have Daze, you can pay for it with your third land. If they Force, you at least traded cards at parity. Tempo demands action.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the draw with Elves: Forest, Forest, Heritage Druid, Nettle Sentinel, Elvish Visionary, Green Sun's Zenith, Glimpse of Nature?
output: This is an excellent keep. You have the Heritage Druid plus Nettle Sentinel engine, Glimpse of Nature for card draw, and Green Sun's Zenith to find whatever you need. Turn 1 Forest into Nettle Sentinel, turn 2 Forest into Heritage Druid, and turn 3 you can potentially Glimpse into a massive chain. The hand has every piece of the combo engine. 9/10 keep.

instruction: My opponent just cast Show and Tell. I am playing Death and Taxes with Flickerwisp, Thalia, and Stoneforge Mystic in hand. What do I put in?
output: Put in Flickerwisp. When their Emrakul or Atraxa enters alongside Flickerwisp, you can use Flickerwisp's ETB to exile their creature until end of turn. Actually, both enter simultaneously, so Flickerwisp's trigger targets their creature. This buys you a full turn to find an answer. Stoneforge is too slow here, and Thalia only taxes future spells. Flickerwisp is your best available option to temporarily deal with their bomb.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Burn: Mountain, Mountain, Goblin Guide, Lightning Bolt, Lava Spike, Price of Progress, Eidolon of the Great Revel?
output: This is a perfect Burn hand. Turn 1 Mountain, Goblin Guide attacks for 2. Turn 2 Mountain, Eidolon of the Great Revel taxes everything they do. You have 9 points of direct damage in hand (Bolt, Spike, Price) plus the creature clock. Price of Progress will likely deal 4-8 damage against a typical Legacy mana base. This hand wins by turn 3-4 against most opponents. 10/10 snap keep.

instruction: It is turn 2. My opponent played turn 1 Aether Vial with Death and Taxes. I am on Dimir Tempo. What is my game plan?
output: Your priority is stopping Aether Vial from taking over the game. If you have Daze, you missed your window since Vial already resolved. Now focus on: Wastelanding their lands to limit what they can cast alongside Vial, keeping Force of Will for Stoneforge Mystic (their best Vial target), and deploying Orcish Bowmasters to kill their small creatures entering via Vial. Do not let them reach Vial on 3 for Flickerwisp or Seasoned Dungeoneer without a plan.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the play with Lands: Exploration, Dark Depths, Thespian's Stage, Wasteland, Crop Rotation, Mox Diamond, Life from the Loam?
output: This is a strong keep with multiple angles. Turn 1: Mox Diamond (discarding Wasteland), Exploration, Dark Depths plus Thespian's Stage. Turn 2: copy Depths with Stage for Marit Lage. You also have Crop Rotation as protection against Wasteland targeting your Depths, and Life from the Loam for the long game. This hand threatens a turn-2 20/20 with backup. 9/10 keep.

instruction: My opponent has Leyline of the Void in play and I am on Reanimator. What is my plan?
output: With Leyline in play, your graveyard strategy is completely shut off. Pivot to your sideboard plan: Show and Tell (puts creatures directly onto the battlefield), Chancellor of the Annex (free disruption), or bounce effects like Echoing Truth to remove Leyline. If you boarded correctly, you should have access to Show and Tell. Entomb and Reanimate are dead cards. Focus on resolving Show and Tell with Griselbrand or Atraxa in hand.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Doomsday: Underground Sea, Dark Ritual, Doomsday, Brainstorm, Ponder, Force of Will, Daze?
output: This is an excellent keep. You have the combo (Dark Ritual into Doomsday on turn 1 or 2), two cantrips to draw into your pile after Doomsday resolves, and Force of Will plus Daze for double protection. The ideal line is turn 1 Underground Sea, Ponder to set up. Turn 2, Dark Ritual into Doomsday, then Brainstorm to start drawing the pile. Force and Daze protect against interaction. 9/10.

instruction: It is my opponent's turn 1. They play Volcanic Island, Lotus Petal, Show and Tell. I have Force of Will and a blue card in hand. Do I counter?
output: Yes, absolutely counter with Force of Will. Show and Tell resolving likely puts Emrakul, Omniscience, or Atraxa into play, any of which effectively wins the game. The card disadvantage of Force (pitching a blue card) is irrelevant if you are dead to what Show and Tell puts in. This is the exact scenario Force of Will exists for. Counter without hesitation.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Death and Taxes: Plains, Karakas, Aether Vial, Thalia Guardian of Thraben, Mother of Runes, Stoneforge Mystic, Swords to Plowshares?
output: This is a dream hand for Death and Taxes. Turn 1 Aether Vial, turn 2 Thalia taxes all their noncreature spells, turn 3 Stoneforge finds equipment, and Mother of Runes protects everything. Swords handles opposing threats. Karakas provides utility against legendary creatures. The only weakness is no Wasteland or Rishadan Port for mana denial, but the creature curve is perfect. 9/10 snap keep.

instruction: My opponent is at 10 poison counters with Infect. Can I still win?
output: No, the game is over. A player loses the game when they have 10 or more poison counters as a state-based action. There is nothing you can do to remove poison counters in Legacy (Leeches exists but nobody plays it). The only way to survive poison in Legacy is to prevent reaching 10 in the first place through blocking, removing infect creatures, or countering pump spells.

instruction: It is turn 1 on the play. I am on Storm with: Volcanic Island, Rite of Flame, Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, Infernal Tutor, Ponder, Brainstorm. Should I go for it?
output: Do not go for it turn 1. You can generate mana (Rite into Ritual into LED) but Infernal Tutor needs an empty hand for hellbent, and you have cards remaining. The line is not lethal. Instead, play Volcanic Island and cast Ponder to set up for turn 2. Brainstorm next turn can help sculpt the perfect hand. A turn 2 kill with better sequencing and knowledge of their hand (do they have Force?) is safer than a failed turn 1 attempt.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Merfolk: Island, Mutavault, Aether Vial, Lord of Atlantis, Master of the Pearl Trident, True-Name Nemesis, Force of Will?
output: Excellent keep. Turn 1 Aether Vial, turn 2 Lord of Atlantis (or Vial it in), turn 3 Master of the Pearl Trident plus Vial in another creature. True-Name Nemesis with two lords is a 5/3 with protection and islandwalk - virtually unblockable and unkillable. Force of Will protects against combo and sweepers. Mutavault also gets the lord bonuses. This hand curves perfectly. 9/10.

instruction: My opponent controls Blood Moon. I am on Dimir Tempo with Underground Sea (now a Mountain) and Polluted Delta (now a Mountain). What do I do?
output: Your mana is devastated. Underground Sea and Polluted Delta are both Mountains now - they only tap for red mana. Your blue and black spells are stranded. Your outs are: basic Island or basic Swamp in your deck (fetch with a new fetchland if you draw one - wait, fetches are Mountains too), or drawing a new basic land naturally. Force of Will can still be cast by pitching a blue card. Focus on resolving what you can and finding answers to Blood Moon.

instruction: It is turn 3. I am on Painter with Painter's Servant and Grindstone in hand, but only 3 mana available. My opponent has Force of Will mana up. What is my play?
output: You need to bait or play around Force. Deploy Painter's Servant first (costs 2 mana) - it is the less dangerous piece alone and may draw out the Force. If they counter Painter, you still have Grindstone and can try again. If Painter resolves, your Pyroblasts and Red Elemental Blasts become unconditional counters (everything is now blue), which lets you protect Grindstone next turn. Never lead with Grindstone since it does nothing without Painter.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the draw with Eldrazi Stompy: Ancient Tomb, City of Traitors, Chalice of the Void, Thought-Knot Seer, Reality Smasher, Trinisphere, Simian Spirit Guide?
output: This is a strong keep. Turn 1 Ancient Tomb plus Simian Spirit Guide for Trinisphere locks out most of their deck. Alternatively, Ancient Tomb for Chalice on 1 is devastating against blue. Turn 2 City of Traitors plus Ancient Tomb gives you 5 mana for Reality Smasher with haste. This hand has multiple lock pieces and fast threats. The only risk is double Sol land life loss. 8/10 keep.

instruction: My opponent activates Sneak Attack and puts Emrakul into play. It attacks with annihilator 6. I have 7 permanents. What do I sacrifice?
output: Annihilator 6 forces you to sacrifice 6 permanents when Emrakul attacks. Keep your most important permanent - typically a land that lets you rebuild or a permanent that can interact next turn. Sacrifice lands first (you need fewer after losing), then dispensable creatures. If you have Karakas, keep it to bounce the next Emrakul. Note: Sneak Attack's Emrakul is sacrificed at end of turn, so you only need to survive one attack. 15 damage and losing 6 permanents is likely game over, but survival is possible.

instruction: It is turn 2 on the draw. I am on Infect with Glistener Elf in play (attacked for 1 poison turn 1). I have Invigorate, Berserk, and Vines of Vastwood in hand. My opponent has one Island untapped. Should I go for the kill?
output: Go for the kill. Attack with Glistener Elf. If they try to interact, Vines of Vastwood with kicker (2G) gives hexproof and +2/+2. Without kicker (G), it still gives hexproof. Cast Invigorate for free (+4/+4), then Berserk (doubling power). Glistener Elf goes from 1/1 to 5/5 with Invigorate, then doubles to 10/5 with Berserk. That is 10 poison (1 existing + 10 this turn = lethal). Their one Island might mean Daze, so lead with Invigorate (free, bait the Daze), then Berserk.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Oops All Spells: Elvish Spirit Guide, Simian Spirit Guide, Chrome Mox, Undercity Informer, Narcomoeba, Wild Cantor, Summoner's Pact?
output: This is a keepable hand with a turn-1 win potential. Chrome Mox (exile Narcomoeba), Elvish Spirit Guide for G, Simian Spirit Guide for R, Wild Cantor for any color. That gives you mana to cast Undercity Informer (1BB) and activate it. Actually, verify: Chrome Mox exiling Narcomoeba gives U, ESG gives G, SSG gives R, Wild Cantor costs RG and sacrifices for any color. You may be short on black mana. Summoner's Pact can find another mana creature. Calculate carefully but this is keepable. 7/10.

instruction: My opponent just resolved Thalia Guardian of Thraben. I am on Storm. How do I adapt?
output: Thalia taxes every noncreature spell by 1, making your ritual chains much less efficient. Dark Ritual now costs 1B for BBB (net +1 instead of +2), and cantrips cost 2 instead of 1. Your options: kill Thalia with a bounce spell like Chain of Vapor (now costs 1U), use Massacre if you are in black (free if they control Plains), or try to combo through the tax by generating extra mana. Thalia must die before you can storm efficiently.

instruction: It is game 2 post-sideboard. I am on Dimir Tempo against Reanimator. I have Surgical Extraction in my opening hand. When should I use it?
output: Wait for the optimal moment. Do not blindly Surgical turn 1 targeting nothing in their graveyard. The best time is in response to their Entomb (exile the creature they just tutored plus all copies) or in response to Reanimate/Exhume targeting a creature (exile it before the reanimation resolves). Surgical in response to Entomb is the cleanest play since it removes their target before they can reanimate it. Be patient and let them commit to a line first.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand with Jeskai Control: Tundra, Flooded Strand, Scalding Tarn, Swords to Plowshares, Force of Will, Teferi Time Raveler, Terminus?
output: Strong keep. You have 3 lands for consistent development, Swords for early creature removal, Force for combo protection, Teferi on turn 3 to lock out instant-speed interaction, and Terminus as a sweeper if you miracle it off the top. The hand covers all bases: removal, countermagic, and a proactive play in Teferi. The only missing element is a cantrip to manipulate draws, but the raw card quality is excellent. 8/10 keep.

instruction: My opponent has Chalice of the Void on 1. I am on Dimir Tempo. How do I play around it?
output: Chalice on 1 counters Brainstorm, Ponder, Daze, and all your cantrips. Your live cards are: Murktide Regent (mana value 2 after delve), Orcish Bowmasters (2 mana), Thoughtseize (dead, costs 1), Force of Will (mana value 5, not countered). Deploy threats that cost 2+ mana. Bowmasters is your best card since it costs 2 and punishes their draws. Murktide is your win condition. Force of Will should counter any additional lock pieces. Do not waste time casting 1-mana spells into Chalice.

instruction: It is turn 1 on the play. I am on Dimir Tempo with Polluted Delta, Underground Sea, Brainstorm, Ponder, Thoughtseize, Daze, Murktide Regent. What do I lead with?
output: Lead with Polluted Delta, fetch Underground Sea, cast Thoughtseize. Seeing their hand on turn 1 is more valuable than cantripping because it informs all your future decisions. You learn if they have combo, what removal to play around, and whether Daze is live. After taking their best card, you can Ponder next turn knowing exactly what you need to find.
